Active Programs
What We Do

What We Offer Neighbors

We provide groceries and hot meals to families and individuals who need them.

Grocery Distribution

Every Tuesday and Thursday, we hand out bags filled with fresh produce, pantry staples, and proteins. Anyone can pick up food. No questions asked.

Hot Meal Service

We cook and serve hot meals on Tuesdays and Thursdays. It's a chance to eat a warm dish and connect with others in the community.

Weekend Snack Packs

During school breaks, we give out snack packs to kids so they have something to eat when school is closed. These are for children in the Rainier Valley.

Get Involved

Three Ways to Make a Difference

Every action, no matter the size, moves the mission forward.

01

Donate

Your financial support goes directly to the people we serve. Even a small recurring gift covers meaningful costs each month.

02

Volunteer

Show up in person and see the direct impact of your time. We have flexible shifts and always need more hands.

03

Spread the Word

Share our mission with neighbors, coworkers, and your social network. Awareness is the first step to change.

1000+ People Served
Our Mission

Why We Show Up for Seattle

Rainier Valley Food Bank started in 2016 because we saw neighbors struggling to get enough to eat. The Rainier Valley is a place where many cultures meet, and many families work hard but still face food insecurity. We fill the gap by being a reliable place for groceries and a warm meal.

Every week, our volunteers sort donations and pack grocery bags. Last year, we provided over 60,000 meals. Our team of about 25 regular volunteers makes this possible.

Serving Seattle since 2016
100% volunteer-led
No cost to those we help
Donations are tax-deductible
